examples.sentence: 彼は頭がいい。ただ、努力はあまりしない。
examples.translation: He is smart; however, he doesn't make much effort.
examples.translationEn: He is smart; however, he doesn't make much effort.
examples.romaji: Kare wa atama ga ii. Tada, doryoku wa amari shinai.

examples.sentence: 外は雨が降っている。ただ、家にいると暇だ。
examples.translation: It's raining outside, but I'm bored at home.
examples.translationEn: It's raining outside, but I'm bored at home.
examples.romaji: Soto wa ame ga futte iru. Tada, ie ni iru to hima da.

examples.sentence: そのレストランは値段が高い。ただ、サービスが良い。
examples.translation: That restaurant is expensive; however, the service is good.
examples.translationEn: That restaurant is expensive; however, the service is good.
examples.romaji: Sono resutoran wa nedan ga takai. Tada, saabisu ga yoi.

examples.sentence: テストの結果は良くなかった。ただ、やり直すチャンスがある。
examples.translation: The test results were not good, but there's a chance to retake it.
examples.translationEn: The test results were not good, but there's a chance to retake it.
examples.romaji: Tesuto no kekka wa yokunakatta. Tada, yarinaosu chansu ga aru.

examples.sentence: この部屋を使ってもいいです。ただし、使った後で掃除してください。
examples.translation: You can use this room. However, please clean up after you use it.
examples.translationEn: You can use this room. However, please clean up after you use it.
examples.romaji: Kono heya wo tsukatte mo ii desu. Tadashi, tsukatta ato de souji shite kudasai.

examples.sentence: 明日から出張で不在になります。ただし、緊急の場合は携帯に連絡してください。
examples.translation: I'll be away on a business trip starting tomorrow. However, in case of emergency, please contact me on my cell phone.
examples.translationEn: I'll be away on a business trip starting tomorrow. However, in case of emergency, please contact me on my cell phone.
examples.romaji: Ashita kara shucchou de fuzai ni narimasu. Tadashi, kinkyuu no baai wa keitai ni renraku shite kudasai.

examples.sentence: 食事代は全額会社が負担します。ただし、アルコールのみ自己負担となります。
examples.translation: The company will cover the entire meal cost. However, alcohol expenses will be borne by the individual.
examples.translationEn: The company will cover the entire meal cost. However, alcohol expenses will be borne by the individual.
examples.romaji: Shokuji dai wa zengaku kaisha ga futan shimasu. Tadashi, arukooru nomi jiko futan to narimasu.

examples.sentence: 図書館で本を借りることができます。ただし、期限内に返さなければなりません。
examples.translation: You can borrow books from the library. However, you must return them within the deadline.
examples.translationEn: You can borrow books from the library. However, you must return them within the deadline.
examples.romaji: Toshokan de hon wo kariru koto ga dekimasu. Tadashi, kigen nai ni kaesanakereba narimasen.

examples.sentence: 暇だって、映画を見に行こうよ。
examples.translation: Since we are free, let's go watch a movie.
examples.translationEn: Since we are free, let's go watch a movie.
examples.romaji: Hima datte, eiga wo mi ni ikou yo.

examples.sentence: 寒いだって、コートを着てください。
examples.translation: Because it's cold, please wear a coat.
examples.translationEn: Because it's cold, please wear a coat.
examples.romaji: Samui datte, ko-to wo kite kudasai.

examples.sentence: 疲れているだって、休んだほうがいい。
examples.translation: Since you're tired, it's better to rest.
examples.translationEn: Since you're tired, it's better to rest.
examples.romaji: Tsukarete iru datte, yasunda hou ga ii.

examples.sentence: 彼女が料理上手だって、みんな彼女の作った料理を楽しみにしています。
examples.translation: Because she is good at cooking, everyone is looking forward to her dishes.
examples.translationEn: Because she is good at cooking, everyone is looking forward to her dishes.
examples.romaji: Kanojo ga ryouri jouzu datte, minna kanojo no tsukutta ryouri wo tanoshimi ni shiteimasu.

examples.sentence: 今日は授業がありません。ちなみに、明日も休みです。
examples.translation: There's no class today. By the way, tomorrow is also a day off.
examples.translationEn: There's no class today. By the way, tomorrow is also a day off.
examples.romaji: Kyou wa jugyou ga arimasen. Chinamini, asu mo yasumi desu.

examples.sentence: この店のラーメンは美味しいです。ちなみに、餃子もおすすめです。
examples.translation: The ramen at this shop is delicious. By the way, their gyoza is also recommended.
examples.translationEn: The ramen at this shop is delicious. By the way, their gyoza is also recommended.
examples.romaji: Kono mise no ramen wa oishii desu. Chinamini, gyoza mo osusume desu.

examples.sentence: 彼は今日は遅刻しました。ちなみに、昨日は欠席しました。
examples.translation: He was late today. By the way, he was absent yesterday.
examples.translationEn: He was late today. By the way, he was absent yesterday.
examples.romaji: Kare wa kyou wa chikoku shimashita. Chinamini, kinou wa kesseki shimashita.

examples.sentence: 映画は面白かったです。ちなみに、原作の方がもっといいですよ。
examples.translation: The movie was interesting. By the way, the original work is even better.
examples.translationEn: The movie was interesting. By the way, the original work is even better.
examples.romaji: Eiga wa omoshirokatta desu. Chinamini, gensaku no hou ga motto ii desu yo.

examples.sentence: 彼女が泣いている。ということは何か悲しいことがあったに違いない。
examples.translation: She is crying. That means something sad must have happened.
examples.translationEn: She is crying. That means something sad must have happened.
examples.romaji: Kanojo ga naite iru. To iu koto wa nanika kanashii koto ga atta ni chigainai.

examples.sentence: このケーキが残っている。ということは、みんなお腹がいっぱいだったのかな。
examples.translation: This cake is left over. That means everyone must have been full, I guess.
examples.translationEn: This cake is left over. That means everyone must have been full, I guess.
examples.romaji: Kono keeki ga nokotte iru. To iu koto wa, minna onaka ga ippai datta no ka na.

examples.sentence: 道が渋滞している。ということは、事故があったのかもしれない。
examples.translation: The road is congested. That means there might have been an accident.
examples.translationEn: The road is congested. That means there might have been an accident.
examples.romaji: Michi ga juutai shite iru. To iu koto wa, jiko ga atta no kamoshirenai.

examples.sentence: 彼が試験に受かった。ということは、勉強がたくさん頑張ったんだね。
examples.translation: He passed the exam. That means he must have studied really hard.
examples.translationEn: He passed the exam. That means he must have studied really hard.
examples.romaji: Kare ga shiken ni ukatta. To iu koto wa, benkyou ga takusan ganbattan da ne.

examples.sentence: 彼は非常に疲れています。というのは、昨夜、車で遠くまで行ってきたためです。
examples.translation: He is very tired. That is to say, because he went far by car last night.
examples.translationEn: He is very tired. That is to say, because he went far by car last night.
examples.romaji: Kare wa hijou ni tsukareteimasu. To iu no wa, sakuya, kuruma de tooku made itte kita tame desu.

examples.sentence: このレストランはおすすめです。というのは、料理が美味しいし、雰囲気もいいからです。
examples.translation: I recommend this restaurant. That is to say, because the food is delicious and the atmosphere is nice.
examples.translationEn: I recommend this restaurant. That is to say, because the food is delicious and the atmosphere is nice.
examples.romaji: Kono resutoran wa osusume desu. To iu no wa, ryouri ga oishii shi, fun'iki mo ii kara desu.

examples.sentence: 試験に落ちた。というのは、十分に勉強しなかったからです。
examples.translation: I failed the exam. In other words, because I didn't study enough.
examples.translationEn: I failed the exam. In other words, because I didn't study enough.
examples.romaji: Shiken ni ochita. To iu no wa, juubun ni benkyou shinakatta kara desu.

examples.sentence: 彼女は独特な趣味がある。というのは、昆虫収集だ。
examples.translation: She has a unique hobby. Namely, insect collecting.
examples.translationEn: She has a unique hobby. Namely, insect collecting.
examples.romaji: Kanojo wa dokutoku na shumi ga aru. To iu no wa, konchuu shuushuu da.

examples.sentence: 明日は休みです。なお、月曜日も休みです。
examples.translation: Tomorrow is a holiday. Moreover, Monday is also a holiday.
examples.translationEn: Tomorrow is a holiday. Moreover, Monday is also a holiday.
examples.romaji: Ashita wa yasumi desu. Nao, getsuyoubi mo yasumi desu.

examples.sentence: 彼は頭が良いです。なお、スポーツも得意です。
examples.translation: He is smart. Besides, he is also good at sports.
examples.translationEn: He is smart. Besides, he is also good at sports.
examples.romaji: Kare wa atama ga yoi desu. Nao, supootsu mo tokui desu.

examples.sentence: この本は面白いです。なお、安いです。
examples.translation: This book is interesting. In addition, it is cheap.
examples.translationEn: This book is interesting. In addition, it is cheap.
examples.romaji: Kono hon wa omoshiroi desu. Nao, yasui desu.

examples.sentence: 私は日本語を勉強しています。なお、韓国語も勉強しています。
examples.translation: I am studying Japanese. Moreover, I am also studying Korean.
examples.translationEn: I am studying Japanese. Moreover, I am also studying Korean.
examples.romaji: Watashi wa nihongo wo benkyou shiteimasu. Nao, kankokugo mo benkyou shiteimasu.

examples.sentence: 彼は頭がいい。もっとも、勉強も大切だ。
examples.translation: He is smart. However, studying is also important.
examples.translationEn: He is smart. However, studying is also important.
examples.romaji: Kare wa atama ga ii. Mottomo, benkyou mo taisetsu da.

examples.sentence: このレストランは美味しい。もっとも、値段が高い。
examples.translation: This restaurant is delicious. But, the prices are high.
examples.translationEn: This restaurant is delicious. But, the prices are high.
examples.romaji: Kono resutoran wa oishii. Mottomo, nedan ga takai.

examples.sentence: 彼女は綺麗だ。もっとも、性格が悪い。
examples.translation: She is beautiful. Although, her personality is bad.
examples.translationEn: She is beautiful. Although, her personality is bad.
examples.romaji: Kanojo wa kirei da. Mottomo, seikaku ga warui.

examples.sentence: 日本は面白い国だ。もっとも、住むのが大変そうだ。
examples.translation: Japan is an interesting country. But, it seems difficult to live in.
examples.translationEn: Japan is an interesting country. But, it seems difficult to live in.
examples.romaji: Nihon wa omoshiroi kuni da. Mottomo, sumu no ga taihen sou da.
